pattern: Instead of tracing every cell down to an ocean, flood inward from each ocean's border against the flow rule; cells reached from both floods are exactly the answer.
hints: Tracing outward from each cell is wasteful; reverse the question and ask which cells an ocean can reach if water flowed uphill from its border.
Run one traversal seeded from the Pacific border cells and another from the Atlantic border cells, each only stepping to a neighbor whose height is greater than or equal to the current cell.
Mark cells reachable from the Pacific and cells reachable from the Atlantic in two separate visited grids, then collect every coordinate present in both.

edge_inputs.description: single row, every cell reaches both oceans
single column, every cell reaches both oceans
all equal heights, water flows everywhere so every cell qualifies
a central peak high enough to drain both ways
strictly increasing toward bottom-right, only corners qualify in part

stress.description: random rectangular grids of varied heights forcing real reverse-floods
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(40, size)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(40, size)))
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 30) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: small value range so many cells connect, exercising both flood fills
